Title: Alternatives to vitamin B1 uptake revealed with discovery of riboswitches in multiple marine eukaryotic lineages

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Multiple marine algae and other domains of life
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pfam PF00474 sodium:solute symporter family; putative membrane transporter
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Thiamine deprivation strongly induces SSSP expression in Micromonas pusilla CCMP1545; SSSP is repeatedly associated with TPP riboswitches and is implicated in alternative thiamine/HMP uptake
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: riboswitch discovery; phylogenetic analysis; qPCR under thiamine depletion; comparative genomic neighborhood analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In CCMP1545, expression exceeded 10-fold over positive controls by day 2 and remained high through day 6 under all no-thiamine treatments
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: THIG; THIE; THIM; THID (operon/genomic neighborhood associations in bacteria)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Thiamine deprivation; no-thiamine treatments with or without HMP/HET supplementation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate transporter target for engineering precursor uptake, especially HMP/thiamine salvage in algae or microbes
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Function not directly proven; substrate specificity remains uncertain
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome and transcriptome searches; qPCR expression analysis; phylogenetics; accession numbers NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Broadest distribution among novel riboswitch-containing genes; only novel riboswitch-containing gene identified in E. huxleyi; authors hypothesize thiamine or HMP transport; previously termed THIV in Methylobacillus flagellatus
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSSP is a widespread riboswitch-associated putative transporter across algae, bacteria, and archaea/eukaryotes. 2. SSSP transcripts are strongly induced by thiamine depletion in Micromonas CCMP1545. 3. Its recurring association with riboswitches and thiamine-pathway gene neighborhoods suggests a role in uptake of HMP, thiamine, or related precursors.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pfam PF00474 sodium:solute symporter family; putative membrane transporter
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SSSF is highly induced under thiamine depletion in Micromonas pusilla CCMP1545 and is linked by riboswitches/genomic colocation to thiamine metabolism
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR under thiamine depletion; riboswitch discovery; comparative genomics
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In CCMP1545, expression exceeded 10-fold over positive controls by day 2 and remained high through day 6 under all no-thiamine treatments
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TH1; THIM (genomic colocation in Micromonas/Ostreococcus lineage)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Thiamine deprivation; no-thiamine treatments with or without precursor amendments
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate transporter for engineering thiamine-precursor uptake or salvage capacity
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome/transcriptome searches; qPCR; accession numbers NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Often located in single-exon genes with 5' UTR riboswitches; genomic neighborhood with TH1 and THIM strengthens thiamine-related assignment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSSF is a novel riboswitch-containing putative transporter in marine algae. 2. It is strongly upregulated by thiamine starvation in Micromonas CCMP1545. 3. Colocation with TH1 and THIM suggests a role in thiamine precursor uptake or pathway support.

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Micromonas sp. RCC299 and related algae
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ATS1 expression rises rapidly and strongly under thiamine depletion, implicating ATS1 in alternative vitamin B1-related metabolism or uptake
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR under thiamine deprivation; riboswitch association; comparative pathway analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In RCC299, significantly upregulated after 4 h in all no-thiamine treatments and >10-fold by day 2; final time point ≥80-fold over positive control and >100-fold in precursor treatments
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Thiamine deprivation; no-thiamine medium with HMP and/or HET supplementation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong thiamine-responsive marker and candidate for engineering alternative uptake/sensing of thiamine-related compounds
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qPCR; genome analysis; accession numbers NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named Algal Thiamine Sensitive 1; present in taxa where classical thiamine biosynthesis genes are reduced; contigs lacking UTRs in some Class I prasinophytes prevented riboswitch assessment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ATS1 is a novel riboswitch-associated thiamine-responsive gene in Micromonas RCC299. 2. Its transcript abundance increases dramatically under thiamine depletion, especially in precursor-amended no-thiamine media. 3. ATS1 likely participates in an alternative thiamine-related process, but precise function remains unresolved.

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Micromonas sp. RCC299 and related algae
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Unknown; no significant similarity to known domains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: UNK1 is induced by thiamine deprivation, supporting a role in alternative thiamine-related metabolism
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR under thiamine deprivation; riboswitch association
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In RCC299, >10-fold higher expression in precursor treatments than positive controls at the final time point under no-thiamine conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for discovering non-classical vitamin B1 salvage or uptake mechanisms
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qPCR; genome analysis; accession numbers NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the novel riboswitch-containing genes most responsive to thiamine starvation in RCC299
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UNK1 is a riboswitch-associated gene of unknown function in Micromonas RCC299. 2. It is strongly induced in thiamine-deplete, precursor-supplemented conditions. 3. The response pattern supports involvement in non-classical thiamine metabolism.

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Micromonas spp., Aureococcus anophagefferens and others
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pfam PF09084; distant from both bacterial SSUA and fungal THI5
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Riboswitch-associated in some algae, but functional role in thiamine metabolism remains unresolved; expression response is weaker than novel genes ATS1/UNK1/SSSP/SSSF
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; riboswitch detection; qPCR in Micromonas
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In RCC299 lower expression than ATS1 or UNK1; in CCMP1545 showed at most twofold increase relative to positive controls
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Currently limited until biochemical function is resolved
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Functional assignment precluded by sequence distance; may not directly participate in HMP-P synthesis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome/transcriptome analysis; qPCR; accession numbers NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors explicitly caution against assigning direct HMP-P synthesis function; E. huxleyi has two SSUA/THI5-like proteins despite lacking THIC and being precursor dependent
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSUA/THI5-like genes are riboswitch-associated in some algae but are too divergent for confident functional assignment. 2. Their thiamine-responsive expression is modest compared with the newly highlighted genes. 3. The study does not support direct classification of these proteins as canonical THI5 enzymes.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Forms the pyrimidine moiety HMP-P for thiamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential cofactor for central metabolism in all life
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of THIC indicates capacity for HMP-P synthesis; THIC-associated riboswitches expand known eukaryotic TPP riboswitch distribution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics/transcriptomics; riboswitch motif and secondary-structure prediction
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Thiamine deprivation context in study; gene distribution analyzed across algae
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker for engineering or diagnosing de novo thiamine biosynthesis capacity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome and transcriptome searches; RNA-seq/transcriptome assemblies generated for several prasinophytes; accession numbers NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: THIC genes with riboswitches were identified in diatoms and prasinophytes; several taxa lacking THIC may still grow if supplied HMP
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THIC was identified as a riboswitch-associated classical thiamine-pathway gene in additional algae including prasinophytes and diatoms. 2. THIC presence/absence was used to assess whether taxa can synthesize the HMP-P pyrimidine precursor. 3. Missing THIC in some taxa supported the hypothesis that they depend on exogenous HMP rather than intact thiamine.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Phosphorylates HET to HET-P for thiamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A eukaryotic THIM gene was found with an associated riboswitch, suggesting riboswitch control of HET salvage in algae
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genome analysis; riboswitch motif and structure prediction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance thiazole precursor salvage in engineered vitamin B1 pathways
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes this THIM-riboswitch association had otherwise been shown only in bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A riboswitch was identified in a eukaryotic THIM gene from G. theta. 2. This expands riboswitch control to the HET salvage arm in eukaryotes. 3. THIM presence helps explain alternative precursor-use strategies in algal thiamine metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Multiple algae including Emiliania huxleyi and Micromonas/Ostreococcus lineages
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Bifunctional hydroxymethylpyrimidine phosphate kinase/thiamine-phosphate pyrophosphorylase (TMP synthase function)
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TH1 family; plant-like or bacterial-like homologs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes late steps leading to condensation of pyrimidine and thiazole moieties into TMP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of TH1 in taxa lacking upstream precursor-synthesis enzymes supports ability to use exogenous precursors such as HMP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics/pathway reconstruction; growth experiments in E. huxleyi with HMP supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: No-thiamine medium with HMP or HET supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports pathway completion strategies by supplying or engineering missing upstream precursor steps while retaining TH1-mediated TMP synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome and transcriptome pathway analyses; accession numbers NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TH1 was collocated with SSSF and THIM in some Micromonas/Ostreococcus genomes; E. huxleyi has nearly complete pathway lacking only known HMP-P synthesis enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TH1 was retained in several algae even when upstream precursor-synthesis enzymes were missing. 2. This pathway architecture supported the hypothesis that some algae are precursor auxotrophs rather than true thiamine auxotrophs. 3. E. huxleyi growth on HMP is consistent with an intact downstream TH1-dependent TMP synthesis capacity.
